Bertunia.Name: Bertunia
arrival: Through an opening amidst the trees, the walls of Bertunia Castle Town rise into view. The smell of woodsmoke and fresh bread drifts out to meet you.
The forest thins and the dirt path gives way to cobblestone as the gates of Bertunia come into sight.
ambience: Merchants call out from their stalls along the main street, their voices blending into a familiar hum.
Children chase each other across the square, weaving between the legs of passing soldiers.
A town crier reads aloud from a broadsheet near the fountain, his voice carrying across the plaza.
The clang of a blacksmith's hammer echoes from a side street, steady and rhythmic.
Pigeons scatter as a cart rolls past, its driver cursing at the narrow lane.

Kacha's Shop.Name: Kacha's Shop
Kacha's Shop.WelcomeText:
A familiar blue-haired woman in a frilly apron greets you at the counter:

"Welcome, ka-ching!"
"I'm Kacha, give me a call if you need anything!"
OtherText: "Be sure to buy something before you leave, ka-ching!"
"Welcome back to Kacha's Shop, ka-ching!"
"See anything you like? Ka-ching!"
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"
ambience: A selection of potions and other items glint behind the counter.
Rows of merchandise line the stalls.
Fellow customers flit about the shop.

Arms and Armaments.Name: Arms and Armaments
Arms and Armaments.WelcomeText:
A stocky woman with soot-stained hands looks up from behind the counter:

"Arms and Armaments. You buying, or just looking?"
OtherText: "What are you working with? I can point you at the right thing for the job."
"Every weapon in here I've swung myself. None of it's decorative."
"Good choice. That one'll serve you."
ambience: Weapons are arranged by type along the walls, organized and deliberate.
The shopkeeper watches you browse without a word.
A set of worn hand-wraps hangs from a hook behind the counter.

Everyman's Clothier.Name: Everyman's Clothier
Everyman's Clothier.WelcomeText:
A tall figure in an immaculate doublet sweeps toward you, arms wide:

"Welcome to Everyman's Clothier — where everyone can shine, darling!"
"What can I do for you?"
OtherText: "You look radiant, darling! Not as radiant as moi, of course, but still."
"The right accessory can bring out the true beauty in anyone."
"Good choice. You'll look fabulous in that, darling!"
"Looking good is a universal right, darling. No need to be shy."
"Hmm, yes… even you could look nice if you accessorise right."
CancelText: "Just browsing? Perfectly fine, darling — the beauty will find you."
ambience: Despite the thoroughly practical stock, the display cases are immaculate.
The shopkeeper is dressed considerably better than anything they sell.
A fresh flower sits in a vase on the counter, aggressively cheerful.

Lantern's Reach.Name: Lantern's Reach
arrival: At the end of the plateau, lies Lantern's Reach, the last settlement on the foothills of Mount Legory.
ambience: The wind off Mount Legory cuts through the settlement, colder here than anywhere on the plateau.
Climbers check their packs outside the guild before heading up the trail.
The forge at The Last Anvil burns day and night — you can feel the warmth from the street.
A notice board outside the guild is covered in curling handwritten requests, half of them old.
The place has the feel of somewhere people pass through rather than stay.

Kacha Outpost.Name: Kacha Outpost
Kacha Outpost.WelcomeText:
A familiar blue-haired woman waves at you from behind the counter:

"Welcome to Kacha Outpost, ka-ching!"
"We've got the basics — everything you need before the climb, ka-ching!"
OtherText: "Be sure to stock up before heading up that mountain, ka-ching!"
"Welcome back, ka-ching! Ready for another run?"
"Is there anything else I can help you with? Ka-ching!"
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"

The Last Anvil.Name: The Last Anvil
The Last Anvil.WelcomeText:
A gruff-looking blacksmith is hammering a glowing piece of metal at the back.
 He looks up at you with a grunt:
"You lookin' for something?"
OtherText: "Lookin' to protect yourself? Or deal some damage?"

*CLANG CLANG*

The blacksmith doesn't even look up from his work...
"I'm busy. Tell me if you gonna buy something."
ambience: The hammering of the anvil echoes through the shop
The warm glow of the forge glints off the merchandise.
Warm embers chase the cold away.

Lantern's Reach.ExitTxt: You start making your way up the trail up Mount Legory.

Mugell Town.Name: Mugell Town
arrival: Rickety wooden platforms rise above the murky swamp water, lashed together with rope and necessity. This is Mugell Town — precarious, but standing.
You climb a rope ladder up from the swamp floor and step onto the creaking boardwalks of Mugell Town, the fog still clinging to your boots.
ambience: The planks underfoot flex with each step, and somewhere below, something large moves through the water.
Lanterns strung between posts cast a warm glow over the narrow walkways, keeping the swamp dark at bay.
A fisherman lowers a line through a gap in the boards, waiting in silence.
The whole town sways faintly in the wind, the ropes groaning in protest.
A child peers over the railing at the dark water below, until her mother pulls her back.

Madam Bernadetta's Mystical Emporium.Name: Madam Bernadetta's Mystical Emporium
Madam Bernadetta's Mystical Emporium.WelcomeText:
An old hag, shrunk and hunchback with age, greets you from behind the counter:

"Hee hee hee, welcome to Madam Bernadetta's, dearie."
"Give a holler if anything catches your eye!"
OtherText: "Hee hee hee, looking for something, dearie?"
"Hee hee hee, here again?"
"Those potions are my own work, dearie."
ambience: The smell of burning incense assaults your nostrils.
Various monster body parts float in vials on the back shelves.
You see shelves upon shelves of herbs and various parts of exotic beasts.

Merrill's Satin Threads.Name: Merrill's Satin Threads
Merrill's Satin Threads.WelcomeText:
A scowling heavyset woman greets you at the door:

"This is Satin Threads."
"Talk to me if you want to buy something, or if you need something tailor-made."
OtherText: "We don't want window shoppers here!"
"Here again, brat?"
"You just going to look, or are you going to buy something?"
CancelText:
"We don't want window shoppers here!"

"Buy something, or get out."
ambience: A large selection of fine clothing lines the walls.
The shopkeeper glares at you.
"We get more than enough spoiled brats around here."

Pages Without End.Name: Pages Without End
Pages Without End.WelcomeText:
A familiar blue-haired woman peers at you over a pair of reading glasses, a spell book open in her hands:

"Oh! Welcome, ka-ching!"
"I'm Kacha — let me know if you need help finding anything. The Fire section is particularly good this season."
OtherText: "Ka-ching! Did you know the Water tome has an entire appendix on tidal theory? Fascinating stuff."
"Be sure to buy something before you leave, ka-ching!"
"The Earth book is a bit dry — no pun intended — but the chapter on mineral resonance is worth it. Ka-ching!"
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"
ambience: Shelves of spell books line every wall, organized by element.
The shop smells of old paper and faint ozone.
The shopkeeper is reading behind the counter, glasses pushed to the tip of her nose.

Mugell Town.ExitTxt: You step out of town into the Lost Swamp, where many have been known to vanish forever into its dull white fog.

Gilded Hollow Circus.Name: Gilded Hollow Circus
arrival: Striped red and purple tents rise out of the dark plateau, torchlight spilling between them. A calliope plays somewhere inside — almost a recognisable tune, but not quite.
You push through the entrance flap. The smell of sawdust and burning oil hits you first. Performers in sequined costumes and porcelain masks move between the tents without looking at you.
ambience: The calliope starts a new tune. It's slightly off-key, in a way that's hard to place.
Somewhere nearby, something large shifts in a cage. It doesn't make another sound.
A performer in a white mask watches you from across the lane. When you look back, they're gone.
The sawdust underfoot muffles your footsteps entirely.
A child in a pointed hat stands between two tents, staring at you with complete stillness.
Gilded Hollow Circus.ExitTxt: You duck back through the entrance flap. The plateau stretches out ahead, quiet after the noise inside.

Lizal-Town.Name: Lizal-Town
arrival: The blistering heat of the cavern drops by a degree — still sweltering, but breathable. Crude tents of bone and dyed leather cluster around the glow of lava pools. Every Lizal you can see has already noticed you.
You step into Lizal-Town. The settlement is quiet — not empty, but watchful. The Lizals don't move to stop you. They don't move at all.
ambience: Every pole and frame here is fashioned from bone or salvaged metal — nothing wasted.
Drakes mill about in a fenced pen at the settlement's edge, watched over by a Lizal with a long-handled prod.
The roar of lava falls carries from somewhere deeper in the cavern.
A group of Lizals hunkers around a fire. They look up as you pass, then look away.
The torchlight here is warm and low, a deliberate counterpoint to the furnace beyond.

Alchemy Class.Name: Alchemy Class
Alchemy Class.WelcomeText:
A man in a thick mantle with many pockets peers at you over a pair of thick glasses, his moss-green hair in complete disarray:

"Ah. A customer. Funding is funding, I suppose."
"Take what you need and leave the gold on the counter."
OtherText: "I'm in the middle of something. Don't dawdle."
"The ethers are standard-grade. They'll do what you need."
"Is there something else, or are you just standing there?"
PurchaseText:
"Good. Leave the rest on the counter."

"Anything else?"
ambience: The classroom smells sharply of reagents and something faintly sulphurous.
The professor doesn't look up from his notes as you browse.
Handwritten labels in a cramped script identify every bottle on the shelf.

Spellcraft Class.Name: Spellcraft Class
Spellcraft Class.WelcomeText:
A woman in neat academic robes looks up from a stack of marked papers and smiles warmly:

"Hello there — Spellcraft is always open to visitors."
"Do let me know if you have questions about any of the texts. That's what I'm here for."
OtherText: "What element are you studying? I might be able to point you to the right text."
"Take your time — there's no rush here."
"The Ice tome is challenging, but very rewarding once it clicks."
"I'm proud of every student who walks through this door. Even the ones who aren't students yet."
PurchaseText:
"Excellent choice. Study it carefully — there's more in there than most people find on a first read."

"Anything else?"
CancelText: "Of course. Come back any time — the door is always open."
ambience: Spell books are arranged by element on tall wooden shelves, each section clearly labelled.
Student annotations are visible in the margins of the display copies.
The professor returns to her marking with quiet focus.

Combat Class.Name: Combat Class
Combat Class.WelcomeText:
A woman in a witch's hat and miniskirt spins to face you, brandishing a colorful scepter:

"Magical girl Ziva here!"
"Here to train? Or just here to bask in my radiance? Either way — welcome!"
OtherText: "Every technique I teach, I perfected in actual combat. Ka-pow!"
"Don't let the hat fool you — these scrolls are serious business. Mostly."
"A true warrior looks fantastic while they fight. That's just science."
"I once defeated seventeen demons before breakfast. The scrolls help!"
PurchaseText:
"Yes! Perfect choice! Train hard and you'll be unstoppable!"

"Anything else?"
CancelText: "No worries! The offer stands whenever you're ready, darling!"
ambience: Practice dummies line the walls, several of them visibly worse for wear.
A rack of sparring equipment gleams near the entrance.
The instructor is mid-demonstration on a dummy as you enter, narrating her own technique.

Runecraft Class.Name: Runecraft Class
Runecraft Class.WelcomeText:
A student in a rumpled uniform looks up from a workbench covered in half-finished projects:

"Oh — um, welcome. These are discarded student projects. Professor said we could sell them."
"They all work. Mostly."
OtherText: "I made a few of those myself, actually. The failures aren't for sale."
"Some of these came from second-years. They're... ambitious."
"Go ahead and browse. I've got revisions due."
CancelText: "Right. I'll be here if you change your mind."
ambience: The shelves are cluttered with labelled trinkets in various states of completion.
Faint rune-light pulses from a few of the items on display.
The student goes back to scribbling notes the moment you stop talking to them.
